ingredients: ['2 cups puffed rice (murmura)', '1 cup roasted peanuts', '1/2 cup sev', '1 small onion finely chopped', '1 tomato chopped', '2 green chilies chopped', '1 tsp chaat masala', '1/2 tsp red chili powder', '1 tbsp lemon juice', '2 tbsp mustard oil', 'Fresh cilantro chopped', 'Salt to taste']

cooking_method: ['Cut boiled potatoes into small cubes and mix them with boiled chickpeas in a serving plate. Drizzle beaten yogurt, tamarind chutney and green chutney generously over the mixture. Sprinkle chaat masala and roasted cumin powder. Top with plenty of sev, chopped cilantro and pomegranate seeds. Toss lightly just before serving to keep the crunch intact.']
ingredients: ['4 boiled potatoes', '1 cup boiled chickpeas', '1 cup yogurt beaten', '1/2 cup tamarind chutney', '1/2 cup green chutney', '1 tsp chaat masala', '1/2 tsp roasted cumin powder', 'Sev for garnish', 'Fresh cilantro', 'Pomegranate seeds (optional)']

cooking_method: ['Lightly soak chiura in water for 2 minutes then drain completely. In a large bowl mix drained chiura with mixed sprouts, grated carrot, chopped cucumber and tomato. Add chaat masala, black salt, green chili, lemon juice and chopped cilantro. Toss everything well and serve fresh as a healthy and light seasonal snack.']
ingredients: ['2 cups flattened rice (chiura)', '1 cup mixed sprouts', '1 carrot grated', '1 cucumber chopped', '1 tomato chopped', '1 tsp chaat masala', '1/2 tsp black salt', '2 tbsp lemon juice', '1 green chili chopped', 'Fresh cilantro']

cooking_method: ['Mix besan with water into a smooth thin batter. Add ajwain, turmeric, red chili powder and salt. Heat oil in a pan, pour small portions of batter to make thin crispy pancakes. Cook both sides until golden and crisp. Break into pieces, sprinkle chaat masala and serve hot as a crunchy tea-time snack.']
ingredients: ['1 cup besan (gram flour)', '2 cups water', '1 tsp ajwain', '1/2 tsp turmeric', '1 tsp red chili powder', 'Salt to taste', 'Oil for frying', '1 tsp chaat masala']

cooking_method: ['Boil or grill the corn until tender. While still hot, brush generously with butter. Sprinkle chaat masala, red chili powder, black salt and lemon juice evenly over the corn. Roll the cobs so the spices coat all kernels. Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve immediately.']
ingredients: ['4 ears of fresh corn', '2 tbsp butter', '1 tsp chaat masala', '1/2 tsp red chili powder', '1/2 tsp black salt', '1 tbsp lemon juice', 'Fresh cilantro']

cooking_method: ['Marinate chicken wings with ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, chili powder, garam masala, yogurt and salt for at least 30 minutes. Heat oil in a deep pan and fry the wings on medium heat until golden and crispy. Drain excess oil and serve hot with lemon wedges and mint chutney.']
ingredients: ['500g chicken wings', '2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste', '1 tsp turmeric', '1 tsp red chili powder', '1 tsp garam masala', '2 tbsp yogurt', 'Salt to taste', 'Oil for frying', 'Lemon wedges']

cooking_method: ['Mix rice flour, grated coconut, melted jaggery, cardamom powder and ghee into a soft dough. Shape into small balls or rings. Heat oil in a kadai and deep fry until golden brown and crisp. Drain on paper towels and serve as a sweet-savory seasonal snack.']
ingredients: ['2 cups rice flour', '1 cup grated coconut', '1/2 cup jaggery', '1 tsp cardamom powder', '2 tbsp ghee', 'Oil for frying']

cooking_method: ['In a bowl mix boiled chickpeas with chopped onion, tomatoes and green chilies. Add chaat masala, roasted cumin powder, salt and lemon juice. Toss well to combine all flavors. Garnish with plenty of fresh cilantro and serve as a protein-rich spicy snack.']
ingredients: ['2 cups boiled chickpeas', '1 onion finely chopped', '2 tomatoes chopped', '2 green chilies', '1 tsp chaat masala', '1/2 tsp roasted cumin', '1 tbsp lemon juice', 'Fresh cilantro', 'Salt to taste']

cooking_method: ['Slice bananas diagonally. Make a thick batter with besan, sugar, cardamom and little water. Dip banana slices in the batter and deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy. Drain and serve warm sprinkled with extra sugar if desired.']
ingredients: ['4 ripe bananas', '1 cup besan', '2 tbsp sugar', '1/2 tsp cardamom', 'Oil for frying']

cooking_method: ['Toss paneer cubes with cornflour, chili powder, garam masala and salt. Heat oil and fry the cubes until golden and crispy on all sides. Sprinkle chaat masala generously while still hot. Serve immediately with green chutney.']
ingredients: ['300g paneer cubed', '2 tbsp cornflour', '1 tsp chili powder', '1 tsp garam masala', 'Salt to taste', 'Oil for frying', '1 tbsp chaat masala']

cooking_method: ['Whisk yogurt until smooth and add sugar, black salt and roasted cumin powder. Lightly crush roasted flattened rice and mix into the yogurt. Garnish with fresh mint leaves and serve chilled as a refreshing seasonal snack.']
ingredients: ['2 cups yogurt', '1 cup roasted flattened rice', '1/2 tsp roasted cumin powder', '1/2 tsp black salt', '1 tsp sugar', 'Fresh mint leaves']

cooking_method: ['Grind soaked moong dal with green chilies, ginger, cumin and salt into a coarse paste without adding much water. Heat oil and drop small portions of the mixture to make crispy fritters. Fry until golden brown on both sides. Serve hot with coconut or mint chutney.']
ingredients: ['1 cup moong dal soaked', '2 green chilies', '1 inch ginger', '1 tsp cumin seeds', 'Salt to taste', 'Oil for frying']

cooking_method: ['Boil or steam sweet potatoes until soft, then peel and cut into rounds. Heat ghee in a pan and lightly roast the slices until golden. Sprinkle chaat masala, chili powder and lemon juice. Garnish with cilantro and serve warm.']
ingredients: ['4 sweet potatoes', '1 tsp chaat masala', '1/2 tsp chili powder', '1 tbsp ghee', 'Lemon juice', 'Fresh cilantro']

cooking_method: ['Marinate chicken pieces with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, garam masala and salt for 30 minutes. Heat oil in a pan and shallow fry the chicken pieces on medium heat until golden and fully cooked. Serve hot with onion rings and lemon.']
ingredients: ['500g boneless chicken', '2 tbsp yogurt', '1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste', '1 tsp turmeric', '1 tsp garam masala', 'Salt to taste', 'Oil for shallow frying']

cooking_method: ['Heat ghee in a pan and lightly roast the mixed dry fruits until fragrant. Add cardamom powder and jaggery or sugar. Stir quickly for 1-2 minutes until coated. Cool slightly and serve as an energy-rich healthy snack.']
ingredients: ['2 cups mixed dry fruits (cashew, almond, raisin)', '2 tbsp ghee', '1/2 tsp cardamom powder', '2 tbsp jaggery or sugar']

cooking_method: ['Beat eggs with milk, sugar and cardamom. Dip bread slices in the mixture. Heat ghee in a pan and fry the bread until golden on both sides. Serve warm sprinkled with extra sugar or honey.']
ingredients: ['4 bread slices', '2 eggs', '1/2 cup milk', '2 tbsp sugar', '1/2 tsp cardamom', 'Ghee for frying']

cooking_method: ['Heat oil and add mustard seeds. Once they splutter, add green chilies and mixed vegetables. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes. Add leftover rice, turmeric and salt. Mix well and cook on medium heat for 5-7 minutes. Garnish with cilantro and serve.']
ingredients: ['2 cups leftover rice', '1 cup mixed vegetables chopped', '1 tsp mustard seeds', '1 tsp turmeric', '2 green chilies', '2 tbsp oil', 'Fresh cilantro']

cooking_method: ['Mix minced mutton with chopped onion, green chilies, ginger-garlic paste, garam masala and salt. Shape into small kebabs. Heat oil and shallow fry until golden brown and cooked through. Serve hot with mint chutney.']
ingredients: ['300g minced mutton', '1 onion finely chopped', '2 green chilies', '1 tsp ginger-garlic paste', '1 tsp garam masala', 'Salt to taste', 'Oil for frying']

cooking_method: ['In a bowl mix roasted gram with chaat masala, chili powder and black salt. Sprinkle lemon juice and toss well so the spices coat evenly. Serve as a quick crunchy protein snack.']
ingredients: ['2 cups roasted gram (chana)', '1 tsp chaat masala', '1/2 tsp chili powder', '1/2 tsp black salt', '1 tbsp lemon juice']

cooking_method: ['Heat ghee in a pan and roast semolina on low heat until aromatic. Add sugar and a little water, stir continuously until it forms a soft mixture. Add chopped nuts and cardamom powder. Spread on a plate, cut into pieces when slightly cooled and serve.']
ingredients: ['1 cup semolina (suji)', '1/2 cup sugar', '1/2 cup ghee', '1/4 cup chopped nuts', '1/2 tsp cardamom powder']

cooking_method: ['In a bowl, mix finger millet flour, rice flour, mashed banana, sugar, melted ghee, cardamom powder, and salt. Add warm water gradually to make a smooth, pliable dough (not too soft). Rest for 15 minutes. Divide into small balls. Grease fingers, flatten each ball, and make a hole in the center to form a ring (sel shape). Heat oil. Gently slide rings into oil. Fry on medium heat until golden brown and crispy (3–4 minutes per side). Drain. Serve as a festive snack during hill festivals.']
ingredients: ['2 cups finger millet flour (kodo ko pitho)', '1/2 cup rice flour', '1/2 cup mashed ripe banana', '1/2 cup sugar', '1/4 cup ghee (melted)', '1/2 teaspoon cardamom powder', '1/4 teaspoon salt', 'Warm water as needed', 'Oil for deep frying']
recipe_name: Kodo Ko Sel (Finger Millet Ring Bread - Hill Festival Snack)

cooking_method: ['Grind soaked chickpeas coarsely (not too fine). Add rice flour, green chilies, ginger paste, cumin seeds, coriander seeds, turmeric, salt, and cilantro. Mix well. Add water little by little to make a thick, sticky dough. Heat oil. Shape dough into small flat discs or balls. Deep fry until golden and crispy (4–5 minutes). Drain. Drizzle with mustard oil before serving. Serve hot with green chutney.']
ingredients: ['2 cups black chickpeas (kalo chana, soaked for 4 hours and drained)', '1 cup rice flour', '2 green chilies (chopped)', '1 tablespoon ginger paste', '1 teaspoon cumin seeds', '1 teaspoon coriander seeds', '1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon salt', '1/4 cup fresh cilantro (chopped)', 'Water as needed', 'Oil for deep frying', '1 tablespoon mustard oil (for drizzling)']
recipe_name: Tharu Bariya (Tharu Community Chickpea Dumplings - Terai Harvest Snack)

cooking_method: ['In a bowl, mix flour, sugar, melted ghee, eggs, and salt. Add milk gradually to make a stiff dough. Knead for 8–10 minutes until smooth. Cover and rest for 30 minutes. Roll dough into thin sheet (about 1/8 inch thick). Cut into diamond shapes, strips, or twist shapes. Heat oil. Deep fry on medium-low heat until golden and crispy (3–4 minutes). Drain. Dust with powdered sugar. Serve during Losar (Gurung/Tibetan New Year).']
ingredients: ['3 cups all-purpose flour', '1/4 cup sugar', '1/4 cup ghee (melted)', '2 eggs', '1/2 teaspoon salt', '1/2 cup milk (or as needed)', 'Oil for deep frying', 'Powdered sugar for dusting']
recipe_name: Gurung Khapse (Gurung Community Fried Pastry - Losar Special)

cooking_method: ['In a bowl, mix rice flour, grated coconut, jaggery, cardamom powder, and salt. Add water gradually to make a thick batter (like pancake batter, not too runny). Spoon about 1/4 cup batter onto each banana leaf square. Fold leaf to enclose batter, folding edges to seal. Steam in steamer for 15–20 minutes until firm. Serve warm as a festival snack. Unwrap banana leaf before eating.']
ingredients: ['2 cups rice flour (fine)', '1/4 cup grated coconut', '1/4 cup jaggery (grated, or sugar)', '1/2 teaspoon cardamom powder', '1/4 teaspoon salt', '1 cup water (or as needed)', 'Banana leaves (cut into 6x6 inch squares)']
recipe_name: Magar Bhakka (Magar Community Steamed Rice Cake - Festival Snack)

cooking_method: ['Grind black and yellow mustard seeds coarsely. In a glass jar, combine ground mustard, roasted fenugreek powder, timur powder, red chili powder, turmeric, asafoetida, and salt. Mix well. Heat mustard oil until smoking, then cool slightly. Pour oil and lemon juice into the spice mixture. Stir well. Close jar and keep in a warm place for 7 days, shaking daily. After 1 week, the achar is ready. Serve a small spoonful with dal bhat or roti.']
ingredients: ['1/2 cup black mustard seeds', '1/4 cup yellow mustard seeds', '2 tablespoons fenugreek seeds (roasted and ground)', '2 tablespoons timur (Szechuan pepper, ground)', '4 tablespoons red chili powder', '2 tablespoons turmeric powder', '1 tablespoon asafoetida (hing)', '2 tablespoons salt', '1/2 cup mustard oil', '1/4 cup lemon juice']
recipe_name: Tamang Achar (Tamang Community Fermented Mustard Seed Condiment)

cooking_method: ['In a bowl, mix flour, brown sugar, melted ghee, egg, baking powder, cardamom, and nutmeg. Add warm milk gradually to make a soft dough. Knead for 8–10 minutes. Cover and rest for 1 hour. Roll dough into small balls or rope shapes. Heat oil. Deep fry on low-medium heat until golden brown (4–5 minutes). Drain. Drizzle with honey before serving. Serve during Rai community festivals.']
ingredients: ['2 cups all-purpose flour', '1/2 cup brown sugar', '1/4 cup ghee (melted)', '1 egg', '1/2 teaspoon baking powder', '1/2 teaspoon cardamom powder', '1/4 teaspoon nutmeg powder', '1/4 cup warm milk (or as needed)', 'Oil for deep frying', 'Honey for drizzling']
recipe_name: Rai Chamre (Rai Community Sweet Fried Bread - Harvest Snack)

cooking_method: ['In a bowl, place tsampa flour. Make a well in the center. Add melted butter, honey/jaggery syrup, and hot water (or butter tea). Mix with fingers, rubbing the flour into the liquid gradually. Add chopped walnuts and raisins. Continue mixing until mixture holds together and can be shaped into balls. Roll into walnut-sized balls. Serve immediately as an energy snack. This is traditional Sherpa food for trekkers and festivals.']
ingredients: ['2 cups tsampa (roasted barley flour)', '1/4 cup butter (or ghee, melted)', '2 tablespoons honey or jaggery syrup', '1/4 cup hot water (or Tibetan butter tea)', '1/4 cup roasted walnuts (chopped)', '2 tablespoons raisins']
recipe_name: Sherpa Tsampa Balls (Sherpa Community Roasted Barley Snack)

cooking_method: ['In a bowl, mix wild yam flour, rice flour, and salt. Add warm water gradually to make a stiff dough. Knead for 5 minutes. Divide into 8 balls. Flatten each ball between greased palms into flat discs (about 4 inches diameter). Heat a tawa or griddle with mustard oil. Cook each flatbread on both sides until golden brown and crispy edges (3 minutes per side). Sprinkle with timur powder. Serve hot with wild greens or gundruk pickle. This is a traditional Chepang community snack made from foraged tubers.']
ingredients: ['2 cups wild yam flour (or taro flour, from foraged tubers)', '1 cup rice flour', '1/2 teaspoon salt', '1 cup warm water (or as needed)', '2 tablespoons mustard oil (for cooking)', '1 teaspoon timur powder (for sprinkling)']
recipe_name: Chepang Khari (Chepang Community Wild Yam Flatbread - Seasonal Forage)

cooking_method: ['Drain soaked lentils. Grind with a little water to make a thick, coarse paste (not too smooth). The batter should be thick like pancake batter. Add ginger paste, turmeric, chili powder, cumin seeds, salt, and cilantro. Mix well. Heat a tawa or non-stick pan with a little oil. Pour 2 tablespoons of batter and spread into a small thick circle (about 3 inches diameter). Cook on medium heat until bottom is golden (2–3 minutes). Flip and cook the other side (2 minutes). Repeat for remaining batter. Serve hot with chutney or as part of Newari samay baji.']
ingredients: ['2 cups black lentils (urad dal, soaked for 4 hours)', '1/2 cup water (for grinding)', '1 teaspoon ginger paste', '1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon red chili powder', '1/2 teaspoon cumin seeds', '1/2 teaspoon salt', 'Oil or ghee for cooking', '2 tablespoons fresh cilantro (chopped)']

cooking_method: ['Drain soaked lentils. Grind with water to make a thick coarse paste. In a bowl, combine lentil batter, mashed potatoes, onion, green chilies, ginger paste, turmeric, chili powder, cumin powder, salt, and cilantro. Mix well. Heat a tawa with oil. Drop 2 tablespoons of mixture and flatten into a thick pancake. Cook on medium heat until golden brown on both sides (2–3 minutes per side). Serve hot with tomato achar.']
ingredients: ['2 cups black lentils (urad dal, soaked for 4 hours)', '1/2 cup water (for grinding)', '2 medium boiled potatoes (mashed)', '1 small onion (finely chopped)', '2 green chilies (chopped)', '1 teaspoon ginger paste', '1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon red chili powder', '1 teaspoon cumin powder', '1 teaspoon salt', 'Oil or ghee for cooking', '2 tablespoons fresh cilantro']
recipe_name: Mashed Potato Bara (Aloo Bara - Newari Potato Lentil Pancake)

cooking_method: ['Drain soaked lentils. Grind with water to make a thick coarse paste. Add ginger paste, turmeric, chili powder, salt, and cilantro. Mix well. Heat a tawa with oil. Pour 2 tablespoons of lentil batter and spread into a small thick circle. Make a small well (indentation) in the center. Crack one small egg into the well. Cook on medium-low heat until egg sets (3–4 minutes). Carefully flip and cook the other side for 1–2 minutes. Repeat for remaining batter and eggs. Serve hot with pickles or as a Newari snack.']
ingredients: ['2 cups black lentils (urad dal, soaked for 4 hours)', '1/2 cup water (for grinding)', '1 teaspoon ginger paste', '1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon red chili powder', '1 teaspoon salt', '8 small eggs', 'Oil or ghee for cooking', '2 tablespoons fresh cilantro']
recipe_name: Egg Bara (Anda Bara - Newari Lentil Pancake with Egg)

cooking_method: ['Drain soaked rice flour. In a bowl, mix rice flour and salt. Add water gradually to make a thin, runny batter (like crepe batter). Heat a non-stick pan with 1 teaspoon oil. Pour 1/2 cup batter and swirl to spread thinly. Cook on medium heat until edges lift. If using toppings: spread beaten egg or minced meat on top, then sprinkle onion, green chilies, cilantro. Cover and cook for 2 minutes until toppings set. Fold or serve open. Repeat for remaining batter. Serve hot with tomato achar or timur achar.']
ingredients: ['2 cups rice flour (soaked in 1 cup water for 1 hour, then drained slightly)', '1/2 teaspoon salt', '1 cup water (for batter)', 'Oil or ghee for cooking', '2 eggs (beaten, optional for topping)', '1/2 cup minced chicken or buff (optional for topping)', '1/4 cup chopped cilantro', '2 green chilies (sliced)', '1 small onion (finely chopped)']

cooking_method: ['Prepare rice flour batter as in #392. Heat pan with oil. Pour 1/2 cup batter, swirl thinly. Crack one egg onto the crepe, spread gently. Sprinkle onion, green chilies, cilantro, black pepper, and chili flakes. Cover and cook for 2–3 minutes until egg is set. Remove from pan. Repeat for remaining batter and eggs. Serve hot with achar.']
ingredients: ['2 cups rice flour (soaked in 1 cup water for 1 hour, then drained slightly)', '1/2 teaspoon salt', '1 cup water (for batter)', 'Oil or ghee for cooking', '4 large eggs', '1 small onion (finely chopped)', '2 green chilies (finely chopped)', '2 tablespoons fresh cilantro (chopped)', '1/2 teaspoon black pepper powder', '1/2 teaspoon red chili flakes']
recipe_name: Egg Chatamari (Anda Chatamari - Newari Crepe with Egg)

cooking_method: ['In a small bowl, mix minced meat, half of the chopped onion, ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, chili powder, and salt. Cook this meat mixture in a separate pan for 5 minutes until browned. Set aside. Prepare rice flour batter as in #392. Heat pan with oil. Pour 1/2 cup batter, swirl thinly. Sprinkle cooked minced meat on top, then raw onion, green chilies, and cilantro. Cover and cook for 2–3 minutes. Serve hot.']
ingredients: ['2 cups rice flour (soaked in 1 cup water for 1 hour, then drained slightly)', '1/2 teaspoon salt', '1 cup water (for batter)', 'Oil or ghee for cooking', '200g minced buffalo or chicken', '1 small onion (finely chopped)', '1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste', '1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon red chili powder', '1/2 teaspoon salt', '2 tablespoons fresh cilantro', '2 green chilies (sliced)']
recipe_name: Meat Chatamari (Masu Chatamari - Newari Crepe with Minced Meat)

cooking_method: ['Marinate meat with ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, salt, and 1 tbsp oil for 1 hour. Grill or roast meat over charcoal until fully cooked (15–20 minutes). Alternatively, pan-cook until charred. Let meat cool, then pound or chop into small pieces (traditionally hand-pounded). Heat 2 tbsp mustard oil until smoking, pour over meat. In a bowl, mix pounded meat with ground timur, red chili powder, roasted fenugreek powder, cumin, raw mustard oil, sliced garlic, green chilies, cilantro, and onion. Toss well. Let sit for 15 minutes to absorb flavors. Serve at room temperature with chiura (beaten rice) and bara.']
ingredients: ['500g buffalo meat (or chicken/pork, cut into large chunks)', '4 tablespoons mustard oil', '2 tablespoons ginger-garlic paste', '1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon salt', 'For Choila Spices:', '2 tablespoons timur (Szechuan pepper, ground)', '3 tablespoons red chili powder', '1 tablespoon fenugreek seeds (roasted and ground)', '2 tablespoons roasted cumin powder', '4 tablespoons mustard oil (raw, for final mix)', '1 head garlic (thinly sliced)', '2 green chilies (sliced)', '1/2 cup fresh cilantro (chopped)', '1 large onion (sliced thin)']

cooking_method: ['Follow same method as #395 (Choila). Buffalo choila is the most traditional Newari version. Use charcoal grilling for authentic smoky flavor. After grilling, pound the meat to break fibers. Mix with all spices, raw mustard oil, garlic, chilies, cilantro, and onion. Let rest 15–30 minutes before serving.']
ingredients: ['500g buffalo meat (lean cut, cut into large chunks)', '4 tablespoons mustard oil', '2 tablespoons ginger-garlic paste', '1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon salt', 'For Choila Spices:', '2 tablespoons timur (Szechuan pepper, ground)', '3 tablespoons red chili powder', '1 tablespoon fenugreek seeds (roasted and ground)', '2 tablespoons roasted cumin powder', '4 tablespoons mustard oil (raw)', '1 head garlic (thinly sliced)', '2 green chilies (sliced)', '1/2 cup fresh cilantro', '1 large onion (sliced)']

cooking_method: ['Follow same method as #395. Chicken choila is milder and cooks faster. Grill chicken for 10–12 minutes until charred. Pound or chop. Mix with spices, raw oil, garlic, chilies, cilantro, and onion. Serve with chiura.']
ingredients: ['500g boneless chicken thigh (cut into chunks)', '3 tablespoons mustard oil', '2 tablespoons ginger-garlic paste', '1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon salt', 'For Choila Spices:', '1 tablespoon timur (Szechuan pepper, ground)', '2 tablespoons red chili powder', '1 tablespoon fenugreek seeds (roasted and ground)', '1 tablespoon roasted cumin powder', '3 tablespoons mustard oil (raw)', '1 head garlic (thinly sliced)', '2 green chilies (sliced)', '1/2 cup fresh cilantro', '1 large onion (sliced)']

cooking_method: ['Follow method as #395. Marinate pork for 2 hours. Grill pork for 15–20 minutes until charred and fully cooked. Pound, then mix with all spices, raw oil, garlic, chilies, cilantro, and onion. Pork choila is rich and fatty, popular among Newars who eat pork.']
ingredients: ['500g pork shoulder (cut into chunks)', '4 tablespoons mustard oil', '2 tablespoons ginger-garlic paste', '1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon salt', 'For Choila Spices:', '2 tablespoons timur (Szechuan pepper, ground)', '3 tablespoons red chili powder', '1 tablespoon fenugreek seeds (roasted and ground)', '2 tablespoons roasted cumin powder', '4 tablespoons mustard oil (raw)', '1 head garlic (thinly sliced)', '2 green chilies (sliced)', '1/2 cup fresh cilantro', '1 large onion (sliced)']

cooking_method: ['Pan-roast or grill mushrooms with 1 tbsp oil, ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, and salt until browned and water evaporates (8–10 minutes). Roughly chop or tear mushrooms. In a bowl, mix mushrooms with timur, chili powder, roasted fenugreek, cumin, raw mustard oil, garlic, green chilies, cilantro, and onion. Let sit for 10 minutes. Serve with chiura as a vegetarian Newari choila.']
ingredients: ['400g button mushrooms or oyster mushrooms (whole or halved)', '3 tablespoons mustard oil', '1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste', '1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon salt', 'For Choila Spices:', '1 tablespoon timur (Szechuan pepper, ground)', '2 tablespoons red chili powder', '1 tablespoon fenugreek seeds (roasted and ground)', '1 tablespoon roasted cumin powder', '3 tablespoons mustard oil (raw)', '4 cloves garlic (thinly sliced)', '2 green chilies (sliced)', '1/2 cup fresh cilantro', '1 small onion (sliced)']

cooking_method: ["Pan-fry or grill paneer cubes with 2 tbsp oil, turmeric, chili powder, and salt until golden brown (3–4 minutes). Roughly crumble or cube paneer. In a bowl, mix paneer with timur, chili powder, roasted fenugreek, cumin, raw mustard oil, garlic, green chilies, cilantro, and onion. Toss gently so paneer doesn't break. Let sit for 10 minutes. Serve with chiura."]
ingredients: ['400g paneer (cut into 1-inch cubes)', '3 tablespoons mustard oil', '1 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon red chili powder', '1/2 teaspoon salt', 'For Choila Spices:', '1 tablespoon timur (Szechuan pepper, ground)', '2 tablespoons red chili powder', '1 tablespoon fenugreek seeds (roasted and ground)', '1 tablespoon roasted cumin powder', '3 tablespoons mustard oil (raw)', '4 cloves garlic (thinly sliced)', '2 green chilies (sliced)', '1/2 cup fresh cilantro', '1 small onion (sliced)']
recipe_name: Paneer Choila (Vegetarian Newari Choila with Paneer)

cooking_method: ['Soak all legumes separately for 8 hours, then drain and keep in separate cloth bags for 2–4 days until sprouts appear (change water daily). Once sprouted, rinse. Pressure cook sprouted legumes with 6 cups water for 3–4 whistles (or boil for 30 minutes). Heat mustard oil in a pan, add fenugreek seeds, then ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, chili powder, cumin, coriander; cook 1 minute. Add tomatoes; cook until soft. Add cooked sprouted beans with water, salt. Simmer for 10 minutes. In a small pan, heat ghee, add carom seeds, pour over soup. Garnish with cilantro. Serve hot during Janai Purnima festival.']
ingredients: ['1/4 cup each of 9 types: black gram, green gram, chickpeas, field beans, soybean, black-eyed peas, cowpeas, lentils, peas (total 2.25 cups mixed legumes)', '6 cups water', '2 tablespoons mustard oil', '1 teaspoon fenugreek seeds', '1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste', '1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder', '2 teaspoons red chili powder', '1 teaspoon cumin powder', '1 teaspoon coriander powder', '2 tomatoes (chopped)', '1 tablespoon salt', '2 tablespoons fresh cilantro', '1 teaspoon ghee', '1 teaspoon carom seeds (ajwain) for tempering']
recipe_name: Kwati (Mixed Sprouted Bean Soup - Newari Festival Dish)

cooking_method: ['Drain cooked kwati completely (no liquid). In a bowl, combine drained kwati, mustard oil, onion, green chilies, roasted fenugreek powder, timur powder, red chili powder, salt, lemon juice, and cilantro. Mix well. Let sit for 15 minutes before serving. Serve as a snack or side dish with chiura.']
ingredients: ['2 cups kwati (cooked sprouted mixed beans from #401, drained completely)', '2 tablespoons mustard oil', '1 small onion (finely chopped)', '2 green chilies (chopped)', '1 tablespoon roasted fenugreek powder', '1 tablespoon timur powder', '2 teaspoons red chili powder', '1 teaspoon salt', '2 tablespoons lemon juice', '1/4 cup fresh cilantro (chopped)']
recipe_name: Kwati Sadeko (Spiced Sprouted Bean Salad - Newari)

cooking_method: ['Arrange all components on a large flat plate or leaf plate (sagun). Start with chiura in the center or as base. Arrange bara, choila, boiled egg, fried fish, black soybeans, ginger pickle, potato pickle, and spiced spinach around. Drizzle raw mustard oil over chiura. Sprinkle timur powder. Place green chilies and lemon on the side. Traditionally served with a small cup of aila or thon. This platter is used for major Newari celebrations (birthdays, weddings, festivals).']
ingredients: ['1 cup chiura (beaten rice)', '2 pieces bara (lentil pancake)', '2 pieces choila (spiced grilled meat, #395)', '1 boiled egg (halved)', '1 piece fried fish (sukuti or dried fish)', '2 tablespoons black soybeans (bhatmas, boiled with salt)', '2 tablespoons ginger pickle (aduwa ko achar)', '2 tablespoons potato pickle (aloo ko achar)', '2 tablespoons green spinach (saag, blanched and spiced)', '1 tablespoon mustard oil (raw)', '1 teaspoon timur powder', '2 green chilies (whole)', '1 fresh lemon (halved)', 'Aila or thon (Newari liquor, optional)']

cooking_method: ['Place chiura in a bowl. Drizzle with melted ghee or mustard oil. Sprinkle timur powder and salt. Mix well. Serve with green chilies on the side and a small glass of aila. This is a traditional Newari snack and ceremonial combination.']
ingredients: ['1 cup chiura (beaten rice)', '2 tablespoons ghee (melted) or mustard oil', '1/2 teaspoon timur powder', '1/4 teaspoon salt', '2 green chilies', '1 small glass aila (Newari distilled rice liquor)']
recipe_name: Baji with Aila (Beaten Rice with Newari Liquor)

cooking_method: ['Drain soaked lentils. Grind with water to make a thick, coarse paste (same as bara #389). Add ginger, turmeric, chili powder, cumin seeds, salt, and cilantro. Heat a tawa with oil. Pour 2 tablespoons batter and spread into a thick circle. If using meat topping, press minced meat onto the top before flipping. Cook until golden on both sides (2–3 minutes per side). Wo is slightly different from bara (often thicker and sometimes with meat topping). Serve hot with achar.']
ingredients: ['2 cups black lentils (urad dal, soaked for 4 hours)', '1/2 cup water (for grinding)', '1 teaspoon ginger paste', '1 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon red chili powder', '1 teaspoon cumin seeds', '1 teaspoon salt', 'Oil or ghee for cooking', '2 tablespoons fresh cilantro', '1/4 cup minced meat (optional topping)']

cooking_method: ['Prepare lentil batter as in #405. Heat tawa with oil. Pour 2 tablespoons batter, flatten into thick circle. Make a well in center. Crack one egg into well. Sprinkle onion and cilantro on top. Cover and cook on low-medium heat until egg sets (3–4 minutes). Carefully flip and cook 1 minute. Repeat. Serve hot.']
ingredients: ['2 cups black lentils (urad dal, soaked for 4 hours)', '1/2 cup water (for grinding)', '1 teaspoon ginger paste', '1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon red chili powder', '1 teaspoon salt', 'Oil or ghee for cooking', '8 small eggs', '2 tablespoons fresh cilantro', '1 small onion (finely chopped)']
recipe_name: Egg Wo (Anda Wo - Newari Lentil Pancake with Egg)

cooking_method: ['Prepare lentil batter as in #405. Heat tawa with oil. Pour 2 tablespoons batter, flatten into thick circle. Press 1 tablespoon minced meat onto the top surface. Sprinkle onion and cilantro. Cook until bottom is golden (3 minutes). Carefully flip and cook other side for 2–3 minutes until meat is cooked. Repeat. Serve hot.']
ingredients: ['2 cups black lentils (urad dal, soaked for 4 hours)', '1/2 cup water (for grinding)', '1 teaspoon ginger paste', '1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon red chili powder', '1 teaspoon salt', 'Oil or ghee for cooking', '1/2 cup minced buffalo or chicken', '1 small onion (finely chopped)', '2 tablespoons fresh cilantro']
recipe_name: Meat Wo (Masu Wo - Newari Lentil Pancake with Minced Meat)

cooking_method: ['Cook the rice/kodo millet until soft but not mushy. Spread on a clean mat to cool to room temperature. Crush marcha into powder and mix thoroughly with cooled cooked grain. Pack tightly into a fermentation jar or earthen pot. Cover with cloth and store in warm place for 8–15 days (check for fermentation smell). Once fermented, transfer to distillation pot. Add water and set up condensation system (traditional Nepali aila distillation uses a metal pot with a bamboo tube). Distill slowly. First distillate is strong (70%+ alcohol). Traditionally served at room temperature in small glasses during Newari ceremonies, weddings, and festivals.']
ingredients: ['2 kg rice (kodo millet or rice)', '200g marcha (traditional Nepali yeast starter)', '6 liters water (for fermentation)', 'Additional 4 liters water for distillation', 'Clean cotton cloth', 'Distillation pot setup (traditional or metal)']
prep_time: 45 minutes active (plus 8–15 days fermentation and distillation)
recipe_name: Aila (Newari Distilled Rice Liquor - Ceremonial Drink)

cooking_method: ['Cook rice until soft, spread to cool completely (room temperature). Crush marcha and mix into cooled rice. Add jaggery if using. Pack mixture tightly into a clean earthen pot or fermentation jar. Pour cooled boiled water over the mixture until just covered (about 4 liters). Cover with cloth and lid loosely. Ferment for 5–7 days in a warm place. Thon is mildly alcoholic (about 5–8%), slightly sweet and sour. Strain and serve cold or at room temperature. Thon is essential for Newari rituals and Jatras.']
ingredients: ['2 kg red rice or white rice', '200g marcha (Nepali yeast starter)', '1 kg jiggery (or sugar, optional for sweetness)', '4 liters water (boiled and cooled)']
recipe_name: Thon (Rice Beer - Newari Mild Fermented Drink)

cooking_method: ['Steam glutinous rice until fully cooked. Spread on clean cloth to cool to body temperature (not hot, not cold). Crush marcha and sprinkle over rice, mixing well with clean hands. Pack tightly into a fermentation jar. Pour 2 liters water mixed with optional raisins over the rice. Cover with cloth. Ferment for 8–10 days in warm place. Chhyang can be drunk as is (milky, sweet-sour), or strained and served warm in winter. It is common among Sherpa, Tamang, and Tibetan communities during Losar and other festivals.']
ingredients: ['2 kg glutinous rice (or regular rice)', '200g marcha (Nepali/Tibetan yeast starter)', '5 liters water', '1/2 cup raisins (optional for ferment boost)']
recipe_name: Chhyang (Tibetan/Nepali Rice Wine - Mountain Festive Drink)

cooking_method: ['Cook rice, spread and cool. Mix crushed marcha thoroughly with cooled rice. If using barley malt, add now. Pack into earthen pot. Add cooled water. Cover with cloth and tie tightly. Ferment for 12–15 days in warm, dark place. Mhicha is slightly thicker and sweeter than thon, traditionally served during cold winter months and Newari festivals. Strain before serving.']
ingredients: ['1.5 kg rice', '200g marcha (yeast starter)', '1 kg barley malt (optional, for sweetness)', '3 liters boiled and cooled water']
recipe_name: Mhicha (Fermented Rice Drink - Newari Winter Beverage)

cooking_method: ['Arrange all pickles in small bowls on a large platter. Place the platter in the center of the samay baji spread. Sprinkle fresh cilantro over pickles for garnish. Serve alongside chiura, bara, choila, and boiled egg. This variety of pickles is essential for a complete Newari ceremonial feast.']
ingredients: ['4 tablespoons aloo ko achar (potato pickle, #620)', '4 tablespoons lapsi achar (hog plum pickle, #413)', '4 tablespoons timur ko achar (Szechuan pepper pickle, #416)', '4 tablespoons aduwa ko achar (ginger pickle)', '4 tablespoons bodi ko achar (black-eyed pea pickle)', '2 tablespoons garlic pickle', '2 tablespoons green chili pickle', '2 tablespoons fresh cilantro']
recipe_name: Newari Pickle Platter (Samay Baji Ko Achar Platter)

cooking_method: ['In a bowl, combine chopped lapsi, mustard oil, turmeric, red chili powder, roasted fenugreek powder, timur powder, salt, and lemon juice. Mix well. Add jaggery if using. Let sit for 2 hours before serving. Store in glass jar in refrigerator for up to 1 month. Lapsi achar is intensely sour and spicy, served with samay baji.']
ingredients: ['2 cups lapsi (hog plum, peeled and deseeded, chopped)', '1/4 cup mustard oil', '2 tablespoons turmeric powder', '3 tablespoons red chili powder', '2 tablespoons roasted fenugreek powder', '1 tablespoon timur powder', '2 tablespoons salt', '1/4 cup lemon juice', '2 tablespoons jaggery (optional for sweet-sour balance)']
recipe_name: Lapsi Achar (Hog Plum Pickle - Newari Sour Pickle)

cooking_method: ['In a bowl, mix ground sesame seeds (white and black), ground mustard seeds, ground fenugreek, timur powder, red chili powder, and salt. Add mustard oil and water gradually while mixing to form a thick paste. Adjust water for desired consistency (should be like thick chutney). Serve as a dipping condiment with chiura, bara, or choila.']
ingredients: ['3 tablespoons white sesame seeds (roasted and ground)', '3 tablespoons black sesame seeds (roasted and ground)', '3 tablespoons mustard seeds (roasted and ground)', '1 tablespoon fenugreek seeds (roasted and ground)', '2 tablespoons timur powder', '3 tablespoons red chili powder', '2 tablespoons salt', '1/4 cup mustard oil (raw)', '2 tablespoons water (adjust consistency)']

cooking_method: ['Grind roasted sesame seeds with warm water to make a smooth paste. In a bowl, combine sesame paste, mustard oil, red chili powder, timur powder, roasted fenugreek powder, and salt. Mix well. Til ko achar should be thick and spreadable. Serve with chiura, puri, or as a dip for Newari appetizers.']
ingredients: ['1 cup white sesame seeds (roasted until golden)', '3 tablespoons mustard oil', '2 tablespoons red chili powder', '1 tablespoon timur powder', '1 tablespoon roasted fenugreek powder', '1 teaspoon salt', '1/4 cup warm water (for grinding)']
recipe_name: Sesame Achar (Til Ko Achar - Newari Sesame Condiment)

cooking_method: ['In a small bowl, combine coarsely ground timur, red chili powder, roasted fenugreek powder, salt, and asafoetida. Add raw mustard oil and mix well to form a thick paste. Timur ko achar is intensely numbing and spicy. Use only a pinch or tiny amount (1/4 teaspoon) as condiment with dal bhat or choila. Store in airtight jar.']
ingredients: ['4 tablespoons timur (Szechuan pepper, ground coarsely)', '2 tablespoons red chili powder', '1 tablespoon roasted fenugreek powder', '1 teaspoon salt', '1/4 cup mustard oil (raw)', '1/2 teaspoon asafoetida (hing)']
recipe_name: Timur Ko Achar (Szechuan Pepper Pickle - Newari Numbing Pickle)

cooking_method: ['Clean intestines thoroughly by rubbing with salt, turmeric, and flour. Rinse many times. Cut into 2-inch pieces. Boil intestines with salt and turmeric for 15 minutes until tender. Drain. In a bowl, mix boiled intestines with ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, chili powder, cumin, coriander, and salt. Marinate for 15 minutes. Heat oil for deep frying. Fry marinated intestines until crispy and golden (5–6 minutes). Drain. Serve hot with lemon wedges and cilantro.']
ingredients: ['400g buffalo or goat intestines (cleaned thoroughly, washed with salt and turmeric)', '2 tablespoons mustard oil', '1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste', '1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon red chili powder', '1 teaspoon cumin powder', '1 teaspoon coriander powder', '1 teaspoon salt', 'Oil for deep frying', '1 lemon (cut into wedges)', '2 tablespoons fresh cilantro']

cooking_method: ['Marinate liver with ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, salt, and 1 tbsp oil for 30 minutes. Pan-fry or grill liver on high heat until just cooked (2–3 minutes per side). Do not overcook (liver becomes tough). Let cool slightly, then slice or chop into small pieces. In a bowl, mix cooked liver with timur powder, red chili powder, roasted fenugreek, cumin powder, raw mustard oil, sliced garlic, green chilies, cilantro, and onion. Toss well. Serve at room temperature with chiura.']
ingredients: ['400g buffalo or goat liver (cleaned, cut into cubes)', '2 tablespoons mustard oil', '1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste', '1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon salt', 'For Spices:', '1 tablespoon timur powder', '2 tablespoons red chili powder', '1 tablespoon roasted fenugreek powder', '1 tablespoon cumin powder', '2 tablespoons mustard oil (raw)', '1 head garlic (thinly sliced)', '2 green chilies (sliced)', '1/4 cup fresh cilantro', '1 small onion (sliced)']
recipe_name: Spiced Liver (Kalejo Ko Choila - Newari Liver Choila)

cooking_method: ['Soak kidneys in salted water for 1 hour. Drain, cut into halves, remove white tubes. Boil kidneys with salt and turmeric for 10 minutes. Drain and slice into thin pieces. Pan-fry sliced kidneys with 2 tbsp oil, ginger-garlic paste until browned (5 minutes). Let cool. Mix with timur, chili powder, roasted fenugreek, cumin, raw mustard oil, garlic, green chilies, cilantro, and onion. Serve as choila style with chiura.']
ingredients: ['400g buffalo or goat kidney', '2 tablespoons mustard oil', '1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste', '1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon salt', 'For Spices:', '1 tablespoon timur powder', '2 tablespoons red chili powder', '1 tablespoon roasted fenugreek powder', '1 tablespoon cumin powder', '2 tablespoons mustard oil (raw)', '1 head garlic (sliced)', '2 green chilies (sliced)', '1/4 cup fresh cilantro', '1 small onion (sliced)']
recipe_name: Spiced Kidney (Girji Ko Choila - Newari Kidney Choila)

cooking_method: ['Heat mustard oil in a pan until smoking. Add dried fish and fry on medium heat for 5–6 minutes until crispy and golden. Remove fish from pan. In the same oil, add turmeric, chili powder, salt, ginger-garlic paste, onion, and green chilies; sauté for 2 minutes. Return fried fish to pan, toss well for 1 minute. Garnish with cilantro. Serve hot with lemon wedges. This is a popular Newari snack with chiura or aila.', 'Nepali Dishes Recipes (421–450)']
ingredients: ['200g small dried fish (sukha machha, rinsed and dried on paper towel)', '4 tablespoons mustard oil', '1 teaspoon turmeric powder', '2 teaspoons red chili powder', '1 teaspoon salt (reduced if fish is salty)', '1 teaspoon ginger-garlic paste', '1 small onion (thinly sliced)', '2 green chilies (slit)', '2 tablespoons fresh cilantro', '1 lemon (cut into wedges)']
recipe_name: Sukha Machha (Dried Fish Fry - Newari Crispy Fish Snack)

cooking_method: ['Wash the buffalo meat thoroughly under running water and pat dry using a clean cloth or paper towel. Cut the meat into long medium-thick strips so they cook evenly. In a large mixing bowl combine mustard oil, ginger garlic paste, turmeric powder, chilli powder, cumin powder, salt, and lemon juice. Mix until a smooth marinade forms. Add the meat pieces and coat them evenly with the marinade. Cover the bowl and let the meat marinate for at least 30 minutes so the flavors absorb properly.', 'Prepare a charcoal grill or heat a heavy iron pan over medium-high heat. Place the marinated meat on the grill and cook while turning occasionally so all sides roast evenly. Continue grilling until the outside develops a smoky char and the inside is fully cooked yet tender. Remove from heat and allow the meat to rest for a few minutes. Slice into smaller bite-sized pieces, mix with chopped green chillies, and serve hot with beaten rice or achar.']
ingredients: ['500 grams buffalo meat cut into strips', '2 tablespoons mustard oil', '1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste', '1 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon chilli powder', '1 teaspoon cumin powder', '1 tablespoon lemon juice', '1 teaspoon salt', '2 green chillies chopped']

cooking_method: ['Prepare all side dishes separately before arranging the khaja set. Lightly clean and fluff the beaten rice so it remains crisp. Prepare spicy potato achar by mixing boiled potatoes with mustard oil, chillies, salt, and spices. Grill or roast the buff choila until smoky and flavorful. Boil eggs until firm and peel carefully.', 'Arrange beaten rice neatly on serving plates or traditional trays. Place portions of buff choila, spicy potato achar, roasted soybeans, and boiled eggs beside the beaten rice. Sprinkle timur powder lightly over the platter for aroma and drizzle a small amount of mustard oil on top. Serve immediately as a complete traditional Newari snack meal.']
ingredients: ['2 cups beaten rice (chiura)', '200 grams buff choila', '4 boiled eggs', '1 cup spicy potato achar', '1/2 cup roasted soybeans', '2 tablespoons mustard oil', '1 teaspoon timur powder']

cooking_method: ['Take fresh beaten rice and lightly loosen it with your hands so it stays airy and crisp. Arrange it in serving bowls or plates. Roast peanuts lightly in a dry pan until aromatic if they are not already roasted. Prepare spicy buff choila separately with grilled buffalo meat and spices.', 'Place roasted peanuts and buff choila alongside the beaten rice. Garnish with thinly sliced onions and a small sprinkle of salt. Traditionally this set is served during festivals and family gatherings together with small cups of homemade aila.']
ingredients: ['2 cups beaten rice', '1 cup roasted peanuts', '1 cup spicy buff choila', '4 small cups aila', '1 onion sliced', '1 teaspoon salt']

cooking_method: ['Place the black beaten rice into a large bowl and lightly rinse it quickly if desired, making sure not to soak it too long as it may become soft. Drain thoroughly and allow excess moisture to evaporate. Add roasted soybeans, chopped onions, and green chillies into the bowl.', 'Drizzle mustard oil over the mixture and sprinkle salt evenly. Toss everything thoroughly using clean hands or a spoon until the ingredients are well combined. Let the mixture rest for a few minutes so the flavors blend together before serving fresh.']
ingredients: ['2 cups black beaten rice', '1 tablespoon mustard oil', '1/2 teaspoon salt', '1/2 cup roasted soybeans', '1 onion chopped', '1 green chilli chopped']

cooking_method: ['Heat oil in a frying pan and fry eggs to your preference. Place beaten rice onto serving plates and top with fried eggs. Sprinkle black pepper and salt over the eggs and garnish with sliced onions.']
ingredients: ['2 cups beaten rice', '4 eggs', '1 tablespoon oil', '1 teaspoon salt', '1/2 teaspoon black pepper', '1 onion sliced']

cooking_method: ['Boil potatoes until tender, peel them, and cut into cubes. In a bowl combine potatoes with onion, green chillies, and coriander leaves. Heat mustard oil slightly and pour over the mixture. Add sesame seeds, salt, and lemon juice. Toss thoroughly and let rest for 10 minutes before serving.']
ingredients: ['4 potatoes boiled and cubed', '1 onion chopped', '2 green chillies chopped', '1 tablespoon mustard oil', '1 teaspoon sesame seeds', '1 tablespoon lemon juice', '1 teaspoon salt', '2 tablespoons coriander leaves']

cooking_method: ['Soak masyaura briefly in warm water. Heat oil in a pan and sauté onions until golden. Add tomatoes and spices and cook into a thick masala. Add potatoes and masyaura with water and simmer until everything becomes soft and flavorful.']
ingredients: ['2 cups masyaura', '2 potatoes cubed', '1 onion chopped', '2 tomatoes chopped', '2 tablespoons oil', '1 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon cumin powder', '1 teaspoon salt']

cooking_method: ['Grind soaked lentils into a thick paste and mix with chopped colocasia leaves, turmeric, and salt. Shape into small nuggets and place under direct sunlight for several hours or until completely dry. Store in airtight containers and fry or cook in curries when needed.']
ingredients: ['2 cups black lentils soaked', '1 cup colocasia leaves chopped', '1 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon salt']

cooking_method: ['Heat oil in a deep pan over medium heat. Fry masyaura in batches until puffed and crispy. Remove excess oil and sprinkle chilli powder and salt while still warm.']
ingredients: ['2 cups dried masyaura', 'oil for frying', '1 teaspoon chilli powder', '1/2 teaspoon salt']

cooking_method: ['Grind soaked lentils into a smooth batter and mix with onion, chillies, cumin powder, and salt. Heat a small amount of oil in a pan and spread batter into pancake shapes. Cook on both sides until golden brown and serve as part of a platter.']
ingredients: ['2 cups black lentils soaked', '1 onion chopped', '2 green chillies chopped', '1 teaspoon cumin powder', '1 teaspoon salt', 'oil for frying']

cooking_method: ['Arrange all prepared items separately on a large platter. Sprinkle mustard oil lightly over the appetizers for aroma. Serve immediately as a traditional Newari starter selection.']
ingredients: ['1 cup beaten rice', '1 cup choila', '1 cup spicy potato achar', '1/2 cup roasted soybeans', '4 boiled eggs sliced', '1 tablespoon mustard oil']

cooking_method: ['Boil the milk and simmer until slightly thickened. Allow it to cool until lukewarm and mix in the yogurt starter and sugar. Pour into clay pots and cover. Leave undisturbed in a warm place for several hours until thick yogurt forms.']
ingredients: ['1 liter full cream milk', '2 tablespoons yogurt starter culture', '2 tablespoons sugar']

cooking_method: ['Simmer buffalo milk until rich and creamy. Cool slightly and stir in yogurt culture and sugar. Transfer into traditional clay pots and allow to ferment overnight in a warm environment until thick and creamy.']
ingredients: ['1 liter buffalo milk', '3 tablespoons yogurt culture', '3 tablespoons sugar']

cooking_method: ['Whisk thick yogurt until smooth and creamy. Add honey and cardamom powder and mix thoroughly. Garnish with chopped nuts before serving chilled.']
ingredients: ['2 cups thick yogurt', '3 tablespoons honey', '2 tablespoons chopped nuts', '1/2 teaspoon cardamom powder']

cooking_method: ['Place juju dhau into serving bowls and top with fresh fruits. Drizzle honey over the yogurt and chill briefly before serving.']
ingredients: ['2 cups juju dhau', '1 banana sliced', '1 apple diced', '1/2 cup pomegranate seeds', '2 tablespoons honey']

cooking_method: ['Prepare a soft dough using rice flour and warm water. Shape the dough into cone-like dumplings and fill with chaku and sesame mixture. Seal carefully and steam for 15–20 minutes until glossy and cooked.']
ingredients: ['2 cups rice flour', '1 cup chaku molasses filling', '1/2 cup sesame seeds', 'warm water as needed']

cooking_method: ['Boil soaked peas until soft. In another pot sauté onion and garlic until fragrant. Add turmeric and cooked peas with water. Simmer until thick and flavorful.']
ingredients: ['1 cup dried peas soaked overnight', '1 onion chopped', '2 cloves garlic minced', '1 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon salt', '5 cups water']

cooking_method: ['Wash dried greens thoroughly and boil in water until soft. Add tomatoes, garlic, and salt. Simmer gently until flavors blend together.']
ingredients: ['2 cups dried greens', '1 tomato chopped', '2 cloves garlic minced', '1 teaspoon salt', '4 cups water']

cooking_method: ['Grind soaked lentils into a smooth batter. Mix with cumin, chilli, and salt. Spread portions in a lightly oiled pan and cook both sides until golden.']
ingredients: ['2 cups white lentils soaked', '1 teaspoon cumin powder', '1 green chilli chopped', '1 teaspoon salt', 'oil for frying']

cooking_method: ['Cook fish pieces slowly with spices and water until gelatin releases into the broth. Add tomatoes and simmer until the curry thickens naturally. Serve warm.']
ingredients: ['300 grams fish pieces', '1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste', '1 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon cumin powder', '1 tomato chopped', '1 teaspoon salt', '5 cups water']

cooking_method: ['Wash buffalo bones thoroughly and boil with water. Skim off impurities from the top. Add onion, ginger garlic paste, turmeric, and salt. Simmer slowly for 1.5 to 2 hours until the broth becomes rich and flavorful.']
ingredients: ['1 kilogram buffalo bones', '1 onion chopped', '1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste', '1 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon salt', '8 cups water']

cooking_method: ['Heat mustard oil and sauté onions until golden brown. Add tomatoes and spices and cook into a thick masala. Add boiled eggs and coat well with the gravy. Simmer for several minutes before serving.']
ingredients: ['6 boiled eggs', '1 onion chopped', '2 tomatoes chopped', '2 tablespoons mustard oil', '1 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon cumin powder', '1 teaspoon chilli powder', '1 teaspoon salt']

cooking_method: ['Slice boiled eggs into halves and arrange on a plate. Mix mustard oil, chilli powder, timur powder, salt, and lemon juice into a dressing. Pour over the eggs and serve immediately.']
ingredients: ['6 boiled eggs halved', '1 tablespoon mustard oil', '1 teaspoon chilli powder', '1 teaspoon timur powder', '1 teaspoon salt', '1 tablespoon lemon juice']

cooking_method: ['Wash the chicken pieces thoroughly and drain excess water. Heat mustard oil in a heavy-bottomed cooking pot until slightly smoky. Add chopped onions and sauté slowly until deep golden brown. Stir in ginger garlic paste and cook until the raw smell disappears. Add chopped tomatoes and continue cooking while stirring until the tomatoes soften into a thick masala base.', 'Mix turmeric powder, cumin powder, chilli powder, and salt into the masala. Add chicken pieces and stir continuously so the spices coat every piece evenly. Cover the pot and cook on medium heat, allowing the chicken to release its juices naturally. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking. Add a small amount of hot water if more gravy is desired. Continue simmering until the chicken becomes tender, juicy, and flavorful. Serve hot with rice or beaten rice.']
ingredients: ['700 grams chicken pieces', '1 onion chopped', '2 tomatoes chopped', '1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste', '2 tablespoons mustard oil', '1 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon cumin powder', '1 teaspoon chilli powder', '1 teaspoon salt']

cooking_method: ['Clean the pork belly thoroughly and cut it into medium cubes with both meat and fat layers intact. In a large bowl combine ginger garlic paste, turmeric powder, chilli powder, salt, and mustard oil. Rub the marinade well into the pork pieces and let them rest for at least 30 minutes.', 'Place the marinated pork into a heavy pan or kadai over medium-low heat. Cook slowly while stirring occasionally. The pork fat will gradually melt and release oil naturally. Continue cooking until the meat becomes tender inside and slightly crispy on the edges. Adjust seasoning if needed and serve hot.']
ingredients: ['700 grams pork belly cubed', '1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste', '1 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon chilli powder', '1 teaspoon salt', '2 tablespoons mustard oil']

cooking_method: ['Wash the buffalo lung very thoroughly with clean water several times to remove impurities. Prepare the stuffing by mixing minced meat with chopped onion, cumin powder, turmeric powder, and salt. Carefully fill the lung cavity with the seasoned meat mixture and tie the opening securely with kitchen thread.', 'Place the stuffed lung into a large pot with enough water to cover it completely. Boil slowly over medium heat for more than an hour until the lung and stuffing are fully cooked. Remove carefully and allow it to cool slightly before slicing into portions. Serve warm with achar or chiura.']
ingredients: ['1 cleaned buffalo lung', '200 grams minced meat', '1 onion chopped', '1 teaspoon cumin powder', '1 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon salt']

cooking_method: ['Slice prepared sapu mhicha into medium-thick pieces. Heat oil in a flat frying pan over medium heat. Arrange the slices in a single layer and fry until the outer surface becomes crispy and browned. Turn occasionally so both sides cook evenly.', 'Sprinkle chilli powder and salt while the slices are still hot so the seasoning sticks properly. Continue frying briefly for extra crispiness if desired. Serve immediately while hot.']
ingredients: ['prepared sapu mhicha slices', '2 tablespoons oil', '1 teaspoon chilli powder', '1/2 teaspoon salt']

cooking_method: ['Clean the buffalo tripe thoroughly with warm water and salt several times to remove odor and impurities. Boil the tripe in water until tender, then drain and cut into bite-sized strips.', 'Heat a cooking pan and sauté onions until lightly golden. Add ginger garlic paste and cook until aromatic. Stir in turmeric powder, chilli powder, and salt. Add the boiled tripe pieces and fry while stirring continuously so the spices coat evenly. Cook for several more minutes until flavorful and slightly crispy.']
ingredients: ['500 grams buffalo tripe cleaned', '1 onion chopped', '1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste', '1 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon chilli powder', '1 teaspoon salt']

cooking_method: ['Wash the buffalo heart thoroughly and remove any tough membranes before slicing thinly. In a bowl combine mustard oil, chilli powder, cumin powder, lemon juice, and salt. Add the sliced heart pieces and mix thoroughly so all pieces are evenly coated. Allow the meat to marinate for around 30 minutes.', 'Heat a grill pan or heavy skillet over high heat. Cook the marinated heart slices in batches so they sear properly instead of steaming. Fry until fully cooked with slightly charred edges while keeping the inside tender. Serve hot with spicy achar.']
ingredients: ['400 grams buffalo heart sliced', '1 tablespoon mustard oil', '1 teaspoon chilli powder', '1 teaspoon cumin powder', '1 tablespoon lemon juice', '1 teaspoon salt']

cooking_method: ['Wash the buffalo tongue thoroughly and place it in a large pot with enough water to boil. Simmer slowly for over an hour until the tongue becomes tender. Remove from the water and peel off the thick outer skin while still warm. Slice the tongue into thin pieces.', 'Heat oil in a frying pan and sauté sliced onions until golden brown. Add ginger garlic paste and cook until aromatic. Stir in turmeric powder, chilli powder, and salt. Add sliced tongue pieces and fry over medium heat while stirring occasionally until slightly crispy on the edges and fully coated with spices. Serve hot as a traditional side dish.', 'Serves: 5', 'Prep Time: 1 hour 30 minutes', "Ingredients:\n('1 buffalo tongue cleaned', '1 onion sliced', '1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste', '1 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon chilli powder', '1 teaspoon salt', '2 tablespoons oil')", 'Cooking Method (Recipe):\nBoil buffalo tongue until tender and peel off outer skin. Slice thinly. Heat oil in a pan and sauté onions with ginger garlic paste and spices. Add sliced tongue and fry until slightly crispy and flavorful.']
ingredients: ['1 buffalo tongue cleaned', '1 onion sliced', '1 tablespoon ginger garlic paste', '1 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon chilli powder', '1 teaspoon salt', '2 tablespoons oil']

cooking_method: ['Thoroughly wash the buffalo spleen under running cold water, remove any outer membranes or impurities, and slice it into thin bite-sized pieces. Marinate the slices with ½ teaspoon turmeric, salt, and a little ginger-garlic paste for 15–20 minutes. Heat 3 tablespoons mustard oil in a heavy kadai or pan over medium-high flame until it begins to smoke lightly. Add finely chopped onions and sauté for 7–8 minutes, stirring frequently until they turn deep golden brown. Add minced garlic, ginger, and chopped green chilies; stir-fry for 1–2 minutes until the raw aroma disappears. Lower the heat to medium, add the remaining turmeric, cumin powder, and coriander powder, and cook for 30–40 seconds. Add the marinated spleen pieces and stir-fry continuously on medium-high heat for 12–15 minutes until the pieces are well coated, slightly browned at the edges, and cooked through. Sprinkle timur powder and freshly chopped cilantro, mix well, and cook for another minute. Turn off the heat. Serve hot with chiura or dal bhat. The texture should be tender yet slightly chewy.']
ingredients: ['500g buffalo spleen cleaned and sliced', '2 onions finely chopped', '4 cloves garlic minced', '1 inch ginger minced', '2 green chilies chopped', '1 tsp turmeric powder', '1 tsp cumin powder', '1 tsp coriander powder', '1/2 tsp timur powder', '3 tbsp mustard oil', 'Salt to taste', 'Fresh cilantro']

cooking_method: ['Cook the egg noodles in boiling water according to package instructions (usually 4–5 minutes) until just al dente. Drain, rinse under cold water to stop cooking, and keep aside. In a large soup pot, heat 2 tablespoons mustard oil over medium heat. Add minced garlic and ginger, sauté for 30–40 seconds until fragrant. Add sliced onion and cook for 3–4 minutes until soft and translucent. Increase to medium-high heat, add shredded cabbage and julienned carrot, and stir-fry for 2–3 minutes so the vegetables remain slightly crunchy. Pour in 4 cups broth, add turmeric, salt, and black pepper. Bring to a vigorous boil, then reduce heat and simmer gently for 8–10 minutes to develop flavor. Add the cooked noodles and simmer for another 2–3 minutes. Check seasoning. Garnish generously with chopped cilantro and green onions right before serving. Serve piping hot.']
ingredients: ['200g egg noodles', '4 cups chicken or bone broth', '1 cup shredded cabbage', '1 carrot julienned', '1 onion sliced', '4 cloves garlic minced', '1 inch ginger minced', '2 tbsp mustard oil', '1 tsp turmeric', 'Salt and pepper', 'Fresh cilantro and green onions']

cooking_method: ['Slightly mash the warm cooked rice using the back of a spoon, keeping some grain texture. Heat 2 tablespoons ghee in a pan over medium heat. Add fenugreek seeds and dried red chilies; let them crackle and turn dark (20–30 seconds — do not burn). Quickly add turmeric and salt, stir for 10 seconds. Add the mashed rice immediately and mix thoroughly on medium-low heat for 5–6 minutes, pressing and stirring continuously so the rice absorbs all the tempered flavors. Remove from heat and let it cool for 2 minutes. Gently fold in beaten yogurt until creamy and uniform. Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve warm or at room temperature as a comforting Newari dish.']
ingredients: ['2 cups cooked rice', '1/2 cup yogurt', '2 tbsp ghee', '1 tsp fenugreek seeds', '1/2 tsp turmeric', '2 dried red chilies', 'Salt to taste', 'Fresh cilantro']

cooking_method: ['Soak chiura in clean water for 1–2 minutes, then drain completely and press gently to remove excess water. In a large bowl, whisk the thick yogurt vigorously until smooth and creamy. Add sugar or crushed jaggery and cardamom powder; mix until fully dissolved. Gently fold in the drained chiura, mixing lightly so the beaten rice retains some crunch and does not turn soggy. Garnish with chopped almonds or pistachios. Serve immediately as a refreshing snack or light dessert.']
ingredients: ['2 cups thick Newari dhau (juju dhau preferred)', '2 cups chiura (beaten rice)', '2 tbsp sugar or jaggery', '1/4 tsp cardamom powder', 'Chopped nuts for garnish']
recipe_name: Dhau Baji - Yogurt with Beaten Rice

cooking_method: ['Heat 1 tablespoon ghee in a small pan over medium heat. Add chiura and roast for 2 minutes, stirring constantly until fragrant and lightly crisp. In a mixing bowl, beat thick yogurt with sugar and cardamom powder until light and fluffy. Add the roasted chiura and fold gently. Soak saffron strands in 1 teaspoon warm milk for 5 minutes and drizzle over the top for beautiful color and aroma. Garnish with chopped almonds. Serve chilled or at room temperature.']
ingredients: ['2 cups thick yogurt (dhau)', '1.5 cups chiura', '4 tbsp sugar', '1/2 tsp cardamom', '1 tbsp ghee', 'Saffron strands (optional)', 'Chopped almonds']

cooking_method: ['Place a heavy pan or kadai on medium flame. Add raw peanuts and dry roast for 8–10 minutes, stirring frequently until they turn golden, become aromatic, and develop small cracks on the skin. Drizzle 1 teaspoon mustard oil around the pan, add timur powder, red chili powder, and salt. Toss vigorously for 1–2 minutes so spices coat evenly. Turn off heat and allow peanuts to cool completely in the pan to achieve maximum crispiness. Serve as a crunchy accompaniment.']
ingredients: ['2 cups raw peanuts', '1 tsp timur powder', '1/2 tsp red chili powder', '1/2 tsp salt', '1 tsp mustard oil']

cooking_method: ['After overnight soaking, boil the peas with a pinch of salt for 15–18 minutes until tender but firm. Drain thoroughly and pat dry. Heat 1 tablespoon mustard oil in a wok over medium-high heat. Add boiled peas and stir-fry for 6–8 minutes until they develop a light crispy exterior. Sprinkle cumin powder, chili powder, timur, and salt. Toss on high heat for 1–2 minutes. Serve warm with fresh onion slices.']
ingredients: ['1 cup dried green peas soaked overnight', '1 tsp cumin powder', '1/2 tsp chili powder', '1/2 tsp timur', 'Salt to taste', '1 tbsp mustard oil', 'Onion slices']

cooking_method: ['Clean brains well and boil in salted water for 8–10 minutes. Drain and cut into small chunks. Heat mustard oil over medium heat, add onions and sauté for 7–8 minutes until deep golden. Add garlic, ginger, and green chilies; cook 2 minutes. Add turmeric and salt, then gently add brain pieces. Stir-fry carefully on medium heat for 10–12 minutes. Sprinkle garam masala and cilantro in the last 2 minutes. Serve hot.']
ingredients: ['2 buffalo brains cleaned', '2 onions finely chopped', '4 cloves garlic', '1 inch ginger', '2 green chilies', '1 tsp turmeric', '1 tsp garam masala', '3 tbsp mustard oil', 'Salt to taste', 'Cilantro']

cooking_method: ['Wash tail pieces thoroughly. Pressure cook with turmeric, salt, bay leaves, garlic, ginger, and water for 45–50 minutes until meat is very tender. In a pot, sauté onion until golden, add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft. Pour in the cooked tail with broth and simmer on low heat for 15–20 minutes. Garnish with cilantro and serve piping hot.']
ingredients: ['1 kg buffalo tail cut into pieces', '1 onion chopped', '2 tomatoes', '4 garlic cloves', '1 inch ginger', '1 tsp turmeric', '2 bay leaves', 'Salt to taste', 'Fresh cilantro']

cooking_method: ['Pressure cook bones with turmeric, salt, garlic, and ginger for 35–40 minutes. Heat oil, temper cumin seeds, sauté onion until golden, then add tomatoes. Add cooked bones and broth, simmer 15 minutes. Finish with timur and cilantro. Serve very hot.']
ingredients: ['1 kg buffalo marrow bones', '1 onion', '2 tomatoes chopped', '6 garlic cloves', '1 inch ginger', '1 tsp turmeric', '1 tsp cumin seeds', 'Salt to taste', 'Cilantro and timur']

cooking_method: ['Pressure cook cleaned trotters with salt and turmeric until very soft (about 45 minutes). Heat mustard oil, sauté onions, garlic and ginger until golden. Add coriander powder and cooked trotters. Stir-fry on high heat for 10–15 minutes until well coated. Finish with garam masala. Serve hot.']
ingredients: ['1 kg buffalo trotters cleaned', '2 onions', '4 garlic', '1 inch ginger', '2 tsp coriander powder', '1 tsp garam masala', '3 tbsp mustard oil', 'Salt to taste']

cooking_method: ['Mix yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, timur, cumin, turmeric, mustard oil and salt into a marinade. Coat chicken pieces well and marinate for at least 30 minutes. Heat a pan and cook marinated chicken on medium heat, turning occasionally until tender and browned. Serve with baji or rice.']
ingredients: ['800g chicken pieces', '1 cup yogurt', '2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste', '1 tsp timur', '1 tsp cumin', '1/2 tsp turmeric', '2 tbsp mustard oil', 'Salt to taste']

cooking_method: ['Prepare or assemble traditional items like spiced choila, bara, fried chiura, boiled egg, and pickles. Arrange beautifully on a large platter or banana leaf following traditional Newari presentation. Serve during ceremonies.']

['Soak black gram dal overnight or for at least 6-8 hours. Drain and grind it into a thick, coarse paste using minimal water — the batter should be thick enough to hold shape. Transfer to a bowl, add finely chopped onion, green chilies, cumin seeds, turmeric, asafoetida and salt. Mix thoroughly and beat the batter with your hand for 4-5 minutes to incorporate air, which helps make the bara fluffy. Heat a heavy flat pan or tawa over medium heat and add 1-2 teaspoons oil. Pour a ladleful of batter and spread gently into a thick 3-4 inch circle. Cook on medium-low heat for 4-5 minutes until the bottom turns golden brown and crispy. Flip carefully and cook the other side for another 3-4 minutes, adding a little oil around the edges. Repeat with remaining batter. Serve hot as part of a festival platter with yogurt, choila, and pickles.']
ingredients: ['2 cups black gram dal (urad dal) soaked overnight', '1 large onion finely chopped', '3-4 green chilies', '1 tsp cumin seeds', '1/2 tsp turmeric powder', '1/2 tsp asafoetida (hing)', 'Salt to taste', 'Oil for shallow frying', 'Fresh cilantro for garnish']

cooking_method: ['In a mixing bowl, combine rice flour, grated jaggery, grated coconut, cardamom powder and 2 tablespoons melted ghee. Knead everything together into a soft, pliable dough — if too dry, sprinkle a little warm water or milk. Divide the dough into small lemon-sized balls and flatten them slightly into discs or shape as desired. Heat oil or ghee in a kadai over medium heat. Once hot, gently slide in 4-5 pieces at a time and fry on medium-low heat, turning occasionally, until they turn golden brown and crisp (about 6-8 minutes per batch). Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels. Garnish with chopped nuts while still warm. These sweets are traditionally offered during Indra Jatra festival.']
ingredients: ['1 cup rice flour', '1/2 cup jaggery grated', '1/2 cup grated coconut', '1 tsp cardamom powder', '2 tbsp ghee', 'Oil or ghee for deep frying', 'Chopped nuts for garnish']

cooking_method: ['Bring water to a boil and gradually mix it into rice flour to form a smooth, soft dough (similar to momo dough). Cover and let it rest for 15-20 minutes. Meanwhile, prepare the filling by mixing grated coconut, grated jaggery/molasses, and cardamom powder in a pan. Cook on low heat for 4-5 minutes until jaggery melts and the mixture becomes sticky. Cool slightly. Take a small ball of dough, shape it into a cone or cup using your fingers, fill with 1-2 tablespoons of coconut-jaggery mixture, and seal the top artistically into a pointed shape. Place the shaped yomari on banana leaves or steamer basket. Steam in a steamer over boiling water for 15-20 minutes until they become shiny and slightly translucent. Serve warm during Yomari Punhi festival.']
ingredients: ['2 cups rice flour', '1 cup grated coconut', '3/4 cup jaggery or molasses', '1 tsp cardamom powder', 'Warm water for dough', 'Banana leaves or steamer cloth']

cooking_method: ['Prepare multiple traditional Newari dishes simultaneously or in advance. Arrange everything beautifully on large banana leaves or steel platters in traditional style — usually with chiura in the center, surrounded by bara, choila, kwati, pickles, yogurt, and sweets. Serve as a grand communal feast during Newari bhoj celebrations. Each guest gets a complete set representing the richness of Newari cuisine.']

['Extract pulp from ripe lapsi fruits and strain to remove seeds and fibers. In a heavy-bottomed pan, combine lapsi pulp and sugar. Cook on medium heat, stirring continuously with a wooden spatula for 20-25 minutes until the mixture thickens significantly and starts leaving the sides of the pan. Add cardamom powder and ghee, stir well for another 3-4 minutes. The consistency should reach a soft-ball stage. Pour the hot mixture onto a greased tray or plate, spread evenly to 1 cm thickness. Let it cool slightly, then cut into diamond or square pieces while still warm. Cool completely before serving.']
ingredients: ['500g ripe lapsi (hog plum) pulp', '1.5 cups sugar', '1/2 tsp cardamom powder', '1 tsp ghee', 'Chopped nuts (optional)']

cooking_method: ['Wash and cut lapsi into small pieces, discarding seeds. Mix the pieces with salt and let them sit for 10-15 minutes to release moisture. In a bowl, add sugar, timur powder, and chili powder if using. Heat mustard oil until smoking hot, then pour it over the lapsi mixture. Mix everything thoroughly until sugar starts dissolving. Transfer to a clean glass jar and let it rest for at least 2-3 hours (or overnight) for flavors to develop. Shake occasionally. Serve as a sweet-tangy accompaniment.']
ingredients: ['500g lapsi pieces', '1 cup sugar', '1 tsp salt', '1 tsp timur powder', '2 tbsp mustard oil', '1 tsp chili powder (optional)']

cooking_method: ['Boil lapsi pieces until soft or roast them lightly. In a blender or mortar, combine cooked lapsi, green chilies, garlic, timur, and salt. Blend or grind into a coarse paste. Heat mustard oil in a small pan until smoking, then pour it directly over the chutney mixture. Mix well and let it cool. Adjust seasoning. This spicy, tangy chutney pairs excellently with bara, chiura, or any Newari snacks.']
ingredients: ['300g lapsi pulp or pieces', '5 green chilies', '4-5 cloves garlic', '1 tsp timur', 'Salt to taste', '2 tbsp mustard oil']

cooking_method: ['Take ready sun-dried lapsi pieces. In a mixing bowl, toss them with red chili powder, timur powder, and salt. Heat 1 teaspoon mustard oil until hot and drizzle over the seasoned lapsi. Mix thoroughly so the spices coat evenly. Optionally, lightly roast in a pan for 3-4 minutes on low heat to enhance crunch and flavor. Cool and store in an airtight container. Serve as a chewy, spicy snack.']
ingredients: ['300g sun-dried lapsi pieces', '1 tsp red chili powder', '1/2 tsp timur powder', '1/2 tsp salt', '1 tsp mustard oil']

cooking_method: ['Heat 2 tablespoons ghee in a large deep pan with a lid over medium heat. Add popcorn kernels, cover immediately, and shake the pan occasionally. Once popping slows down, remove from heat. While still hot, drizzle remaining ghee if needed and sprinkle timur powder, chili powder, and salt. Toss vigorously until the spices coat all the popcorn evenly. Serve immediately as a uniquely Nepali-Newari flavored snack.']
ingredients: ['1/2 cup popcorn kernels', '2-3 tbsp ghee', '1 tsp timur powder', '1/2 tsp red chili powder', '1/2 tsp salt']

cooking_method: ['Dry roast peanuts in a heavy pan on medium heat for 8-10 minutes, stirring continuously until aromatic and slightly darker. Remove and set aside. In the same pan, heat mustard oil until hot. Add the roasted peanuts back along with chili powder, timur powder, and salt. Toss on low heat for 1-2 minutes until spices are well absorbed. Cool completely before serving for maximum crunch.']
ingredients: ['2 cups raw peanuts', '1 tsp red chili powder', '1/2 tsp timur powder', '1/2 tsp salt', '1 tbsp mustard oil']

cooking_method: ['Wash mustard greens thoroughly and chop finely. Heat mustard oil in a wok until smoking. Add fenugreek seeds and dried red chilies; let them crackle. Add crushed garlic and a pinch of asafoetida, stir for 20 seconds. Add chopped greens and salt. Stir-fry on high heat for 5-7 minutes until the greens wilt and are cooked but still retain vibrant green color. Serve hot with chiura or dal bhat.']
ingredients: ['500g fresh mustard greens (tori ko saag)', '2 dried red chilies', '5 garlic cloves crushed', '1 tsp fenugreek seeds', '2 tbsp mustard oil', 'Salt to taste', 'Pinch of asafoetida']

cooking_method: ['Wash and roughly chop the greens. Heat mustard oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter. Add sliced garlic and green chilies, sauté for 30-40 seconds. Add chopped greens and salt. Cook on high heat for 4-6 minutes, stirring frequently, until the greens are just wilted but not overcooked. Serve immediately as a side dish.']
ingredients: ['400g mixed leafy greens (spinach, mustard, etc.)', '4 cloves garlic', '2-3 green chilies', '1 tsp cumin seeds', '2 tbsp mustard oil', 'Salt to taste']

cooking_method: ['Wash and cut vegetables into bite-sized pieces. Mix thoroughly with salt, mustard seeds, and timur. Pack tightly into a clean glass jar, pressing down firmly to release juices. Cover with a little mustard oil on top. Seal and let ferment in a warm place for 3-5 days, tasting daily after day 2. Once sour enough, refrigerate. Use as pickle or side.']
ingredients: ['500g mixed vegetables (radish, cauliflower, carrot)', '2-3 tbsp salt', '1 tsp mustard seeds', '1 tsp timur', 'Mustard oil']

cooking_method: ['Chop tomatoes or radish and green chilies finely. Mix with salt and timur powder in a bowl. Heat mustard oil in a small pan until it smokes heavily. Carefully pour the hot oil over the chopped mixture. Stir well to combine. Let it rest for at least 30 minutes so flavors infuse. Serve with any Newari meal.']
ingredients: ['4 ripe tomatoes or 1 radish', '5-6 green chilies', '1 tsp timur powder', '1 tsp salt', '4 tbsp mustard oil']

cooking_method: ['Heat 1 tablespoon ghee in a pan over low heat. Add mixed nuts and dried fruits. Stir and roast gently for 4-5 minutes until fragrant. Sprinkle timur powder and a pinch of salt. Toss well and remove from heat. Cool slightly before serving as a healthy, energy-rich Newari snack.', 'All recipes from 464-480 are now fully updated with enhanced details.\nLet me know if you want the next set or any further modifications!']
ingredients: ['1 cup mixed dried fruits (apple, lapsi, mango)', '1/2 cup mixed nuts', '1 tbsp ghee', '1/2 tsp timur', 'Pinch of salt']

cooking_method: ['Bring a large pot of water to boil and cook the egg noodles according to package instructions until al dente (usually 4-6 minutes). Drain, rinse with cold water to prevent sticking, and set aside. In a large soup pot, heat 2 tablespoons mustard oil over medium heat. Add minced garlic, ginger, and sliced onion; sauté for 2-3 minutes until the onions turn soft and fragrant. Add slit green chilies and chopped tomato (if using) and cook for another 1-2 minutes. Increase heat to medium-high, add shredded cabbage and julienned carrot, then stir-fry for 2-3 minutes so the vegetables remain slightly crunchy. Pour in 4 cups of broth, add turmeric powder, cumin powder, black pepper, and salt. Bring the soup to a rolling boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer gently for 8-10 minutes to allow all the flavors to meld beautifully. Add the cooked noodles into the simmering soup and cook for 2-3 more minutes. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and a dash of soy sauce if desired. Turn off the heat and garnish generously with chopped fresh cilantro and green onions. Serve piping hot in deep bowls for a comforting and hearty meal.']
ingredients: ['200g egg noodles or Tibetan noodles', '4 cups chicken or vegetable broth', '1 cup shredded cabbage', '1 carrot julienned', '1 onion thinly sliced', '4 cloves garlic minced', '1 inch ginger minced', '2 green chilies slit', '1 tsp turmeric powder', '1 tsp cumin powder', '1/2 tsp black pepper', '2 tbsp mustard oil or vegetable oil', 'Salt to taste', 'Fresh cilantro and green onions chopped', '1 tomato chopped (optional)', 'Soy sauce to taste']

cooking_method: ['Cook noodles according to package instructions. Drain and set aside. Heat oil in a large pot, add ginger and garlic; sauté 1 minute. Add onion; cook until translucent. Add carrot, cabbage, bell peppers, green beans; stir-fry for 2 minutes. Add vegetable broth, soy sauce, tomato paste, chili powder, turmeric, and salt. Bring to boil, then simmer for 10 minutes. Add cooked noodles, cilantro, and green onions. Heat through for 2 minutes. Serve hot.']
ingredients: ['200g vegetarian noodles (whole wheat or eggless)', '2 tablespoons vegetable oil', '1 tablespoon ginger (minced)', '2 cloves garlic (minced)', '1 small onion (sliced)', '1 carrot (julienned)', '1/2 cup cabbage (shredded)', '1/4 cup bell peppers (sliced)', '1/4 cup green beans (chopped)', '5 cups vegetable broth (or water)', '2 tablespoons soy sauce', '1 tablespoon tomato paste', '1 teaspoon red chili powder', '1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder', '1 teaspoon salt', '2 tablespoons fresh cilantro (chopped)', '2 green onions (chopped)']

cooking_method: ['Cook noodles, drain, set aside. Heat oil in a pot, add ginger and garlic; sauté 1 minute. Add buffalo meat; cook until browned (5–7 minutes).
